ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |name = 河原淵神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawarabuchi shrine, Funae.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[船江 (伊勢市)|船江]]一丁目10-140 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 56.0 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 52.2 |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=outer garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |name = 河原淵神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawarabuchi shrine, Funae.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[船江 (伊勢市)|船江]]一丁目10-140 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 56.0 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 52.2 |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naiku)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. {{Infobox religious building |name = 河原淵神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawarabuchi shrine, Funae.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[船江 (伊勢市)|船江]]一丁目10-140 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 56.0 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 52.2 |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naikū)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |name = 河原淵神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawarabuchi shrine, Funae.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[船江 (伊勢市)|船江]]一丁目10-140 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 56.0 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 52.2 |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|WD=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|WD=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naikū)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|WD=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]]

Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|qid=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|qid=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naikū)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|qid=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|qid=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]]

{{Infobox religious building |name = 河原淵神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawarabuchi shrine, Funae.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[船江 (伊勢市)|船江]]一丁目10-140 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 56.0 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 52.2 |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qid=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|qid=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|qid=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[Kawara Shrine (Naikū)|川原神社 (伊勢市)]] - 河原淵神社が川原神社の旧社地だったとする説がある。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0404.html 河原淵神社（かわらぶちじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|河原淵神社|version=84566091|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Kawarabuchi Shrine (Q17209232)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kawarabuchi Shrine|lt=Kawarabuchi Shrine|ja|河原淵神社|qid=Q17209232|1=Kawarabuchi Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|qid=Q11633343|1=Toyōke Daijingū}} || — || {{coord|34.498910|136.714503|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181154 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17209232}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17209232]] [[ja:河原淵神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]]

{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事 = }} {{Infobox religious building |name = 船江上社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Funae Kami-no-yashiro.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]船江一丁目10-140 |位置 = {{coord|34|29|56.2|N|136|42|51.9|E|display=inline}} |deity = 澤姫命 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = [[Sengoku period]]以降 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[9月15日]] |神事 = }} '''Kawarabuchi Shrine''' (河原淵神社, Kawarabuchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{ill|Geku|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633343}}) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This article also covers the former village shrine, '''Funae Kamisha''' (船江上社), located on the same grounds as Kawarabuchi Shrine. == Overview == Kawarabuchi Shrine is located at 1-10-140 Funae, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.44 The grounds originally belonged to Funae Kamisha, and following historical research within Ise Grand Shrine after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was established on the site of Funae Kamisha.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.910 The precinct of Funae Kamisha covers approximately 765 tsubo (about 2,528.9 m2), including an {{ill|outer garden|ja|外苑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outer garden|1=Q11430523|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430523}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.915 Kawarabuchi Shrine's area is 7 are (about 694.2 m2).Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, p.12 It Ranks 12th among the 16 sessha of Geku.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.1, pp.9, 11-12 Unlike most sessha, which do not have {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qid=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory boxes), Kawarabuchi Shrine has one. The main shrine is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (fence) and features one {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.376 The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is '''Sawahime no Mikoto''' (澤姫命), revered as a {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of water.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63 There is speculation that the deity might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nakisawe|ja|鳴澤女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakisawe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in some records. == Funae Kamisha == Funae Kamisha serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) for the local area of Funae in Ise. Its official shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. Historically known as "Hachiman-gu" by locals, it is not directly linked to {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): vol.2, p.909 Initially, the deity was either unknown or associated with {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, but in 1913 (Taisho 2), it was unified with Kawarabuchi Shrine’s deity, Sawahime no Mikoto. The grounds also feature various subordinate shrines. == Oboro Pond == '''Oboro Pond''' (朧ヶ池) lies within the shrine precinct and is believed to be inhabited by a {{ill|dragon god|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragon god|1=Dragon god|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1206058}}. Historically connected to the {{ill|Miya River (Mie)|en|Miya River (Mie)|qid=Q6884164|lt=Miyagawa River|1=Miya River (Mie)}} and {{ill|Seta River (Mie)|en|Seta River (Mie)|qid=Q7456322|lt=Setagawa River|1=Seta River (Mie)}}, it influenced the naming of the "Funae" area. == History == While the exact founding date is unknown, Kawarabuchi Shrine appears in the "Toyōke Daijingū Ritual Records" compiled in 804 (Enryaku 23), indicating its ancient origins. It was reconstructed in the Edo period and relocated in 1878 due to flood risks. == Festivals == Annual festivals include the {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} on September 15 and a {{ill|Shinji (ritual)|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji (ritual)|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588671}} on March 1. The "Fuchi Festival" involves {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} performances and ritual offerings floated on Oboro Pond. == Access == * Public transport: From {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus to "Asahidori" stop, then a 3-minute walk. * By car: 8 minutes north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == ;References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p.
